methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $22M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Darkhive is a Series A-stage defense and security company that has raised $22M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparables universe of 10 peers—2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries operating in the same sector and stage.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy base of $22M and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A to next-stage advancement, implies a valuation of $44M. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage comparables—Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M)—yields a median of $300M, with a range of $150M to $500M.

Triangulation across these two methods produces a valuation range of $37M (low) to $345M (high), with a median point of $172M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around Darkhive's relative competitive positioning and growth trajectory versus established peers. Stage-progression implies significantly lower value, while peer medians reflect outsized valuations in the Defense & Security venture market. Key uncertainties include customer traction, technology differentiation, and whether Darkhive's profile more closely resembles the lower quartile (Ares) or upper quartile (Stark Defence) of the peer set. Further diligence on revenue run-rate and customer concentration would narrow this range materially.
